rulings.Case Title: RUBY JOYNER VS. KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Mission Stucco Company - Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 11, 2016 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Mission Stucco Company's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that decedent Claude Joyner was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Ersie Joyner's deposition testimony and plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of presenting evidence that would require a reasonable trier of fact not to find any underlying material fact more likely than not. Even if the declaration of Gilbert Hernandez is admissible in its entirety, it fails to conclusively negate an element of plaintiff's causes of action. At most, when read in conjunction with Ersie Joyner's deposition testimony, it merely creates a triable issue of material fact. However even had defendant sustained its initial burden, the deposition testimony of Ersie Joyner, Gilbert Hernandez and James Well, the declaration of Arthur Woodman and defendant's responses to General Order no. 129 interrogatories would create a triable issue whether decedent was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs' objections to Mr. Hernandez's declaration are overruled. Defendant's objections to the August 2014 Ersie Joyner testimony are sustained and its objections to the September 2015 Ersie Joyner testimony (55:13-18) are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: OSCEOLA HEATH ET AL VS. 3M COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 11, 2016 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Owens-Illinois, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' negligence and strict liability causes of action and claim for punitive damages are denied. Defendant failed to satisfy its burden of production of making a prima facie case that it is entitled to prevail on its government contractor defense as to plaintiffs' claims based on failure to warn. Defendant satisfied its burden of production of making a prima facie case that it is entitled to prevail on its government contractor defense as to plaintiffs' claims based on design defect and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue on that topic. However the motion as a whole must be denied because defendant did not separately seek summary adjudication of plaintiffs' causes of action based solely on design defect and excluding failure to warn. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)

rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 11, 2016 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Fryer-Knowles, Inc.'s, a Washington corporation, motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication is continued on the court's own motion to February 17, 2016. = (503/GLW)
